definition: To shorten a word, phrase, or text
exampleSentence: The word 'doctor' is often abbreviated to 'Dr.'
exampleTranslation: “doctor”一词通常被缩写为“Dr.”

definition: To officially end a law, system, or institution
exampleSentence: The government voted to abolish the outdated policy.
exampleTranslation: 政府投票废除了过时的政策。

definition: To take in or soak up energy, liquid, or knowledge
exampleSentence: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
exampleTranslation: 植物从大气中吸收二氧化碳。

definition: To deliberately choose not to do or have something
exampleSentence: Several delegates abstained from the vote.
exampleTranslation: 几位代表在投票中弃权了。

definition: Existing in thought or as an idea rather than physical
exampleSentence: The concept of justice is highly abstract.
exampleTranslation: 正义的概念是高度抽象的。

definition: A very large quantity of something; plentifulness
exampleSentence: The region is known for its abundance of natural resources.
exampleTranslation: 该地区以其丰富的自然资源而闻名。

definition: Existing in large quantities; more than enough
exampleSentence: Fossil evidence is abundant in this geological layer.
exampleTranslation: 化石证据在这个地质层中非常丰富。

definition: Relating to education, scholarship, or scholarly pursuits
exampleSentence: Her academic achievements earned her a full scholarship.
exampleTranslation: 她的学术成就为她赢得了全额奖学金。

definition: To increase in speed or rate of occurrence
exampleSentence: Global warming may accelerate the melting of polar ice.
exampleTranslation: 全球变暖可能加速极地冰层的融化。

definition: Able to be reached, entered, or easily understood
exampleSentence: The professor made complex theories accessible to all students.
exampleTranslation: 教授让所有学生都能理解复杂的理论。

definition: To provide space or adjust to suit particular needs
exampleSentence: The auditorium can accommodate up to 500 attendees.
exampleTranslation: 礼堂最多可容纳500名与会者。

definition: To go somewhere or occur together with something
exampleSentence: Rising temperatures often accompany increased humidity.
exampleTranslation: 温度升高通常伴随着湿度增加。

definition: To achieve or complete something successfully
exampleSentence: The research team accomplished their goal ahead of schedule.
exampleTranslation: 研究团队提前完成了他们的目标。

definition: An official agreement or treaty between parties
exampleSentence: The two nations signed an accord on climate change.
exampleTranslation: 两国签署了一项关于气候变化的协议。

definition: To gather together or acquire over time
exampleSentence: Sediment accumulates at the bottom of riverbeds.
exampleTranslation: 沉积物在河床底部积聚。

definition: Correct in all details; free from errors
exampleSentence: Accurate data is essential for scientific research.
exampleTranslation: 准确的数据对科学研究至关重要。

definition: To successfully reach a desired objective through effort
exampleSentence: Students who set clear goals are more likely to achieve them.
exampleTranslation: 设定明确目标的学生更有可能实现目标。

definition: A chemical substance with a pH lower than seven
exampleSentence: Acid rain can severely damage forest ecosystems.
exampleTranslation: 酸雨会严重破坏森林生态系统。

definition: To accept or recognize the existence or truth of something
exampleSentence: The researcher acknowledged the limitations of the study.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员承认了该研究的局限性。

definition: To obtain or gain possession of something
exampleSentence: Children acquire language skills through social interaction.
exampleTranslation: 儿童通过社交互动习得语言技能。

definition: To make something active or operative
exampleSentence: Certain enzymes activate chemical reactions in cells.
exampleTranslation: 某些酶会激活细胞中的化学反应。

definition: To adjust to new conditions or environment
exampleSentence: Species must adapt to changing environmental conditions.
exampleTranslation: 物种必须适应不断变化的环境条件。

definition: Sufficient for a specific need or requirement
exampleSentence: The study lacked adequate sample sizes for valid conclusions.
exampleTranslation: 该研究缺乏足够的样本量来得出有效结论。

definition: The action of sticking to a surface or substance
exampleSentence: The adhesion of water molecules to surfaces enables capillary action.
exampleTranslation: 水分子对表面的附着使毛细作用成为可能。

definition: Next to or near something without intervening space
exampleSentence: The library is adjacent to the main lecture hall.
exampleTranslation: 图书馆毗邻主讲堂。

definition: To alter or modify slightly to achieve a desired result
exampleSentence: Researchers adjusted the variables to improve accuracy.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员调整了变量以提高准确性。

definition: To manage, direct, or be in charge of an organization
exampleSentence: The agency administers federal environmental regulations.
exampleTranslation: 该机构负责管理联邦环境法规。

definition: A young person developing from child to adult
exampleSentence: Adolescents undergo significant cognitive development.
exampleTranslation: 青少年经历重大的认知发展。

definition: The arrival or emergence of a notable person or thing
exampleSentence: The advent of the internet transformed global communication.
exampleTranslation: 互联网的出现改变了全球通信方式。

definition: Difficulties or misfortune; an adverse condition
exampleSentence: Species that survive adversity often develop unique adaptations.
exampleTranslation: 在逆境中存活的物种往往发展出独特的适应性。

definition: To publicly recommend or support a particular cause
exampleSentence: Many scientists advocate for increased funding in research.
exampleTranslation: 许多科学家提倡增加研究经费。

definition: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty
exampleSentence: The aesthetic qualities of Renaissance art are widely admired.
exampleTranslation: 文艺复兴时期艺术的美学品质广受赞赏。

definition: To have an influence on or cause a change in something
exampleSentence: Deforestation significantly affects local climate patterns.
exampleTranslation: 森林砍伐显著影响当地气候模式。

definition: To officially attach or connect to an organization
exampleSentence: The research center is affiliated with the university.
exampleTranslation: 该研究中心隶属于该大学。

definition: A list of items to be discussed at a meeting; a plan
exampleSentence: Climate change is high on the international policy agenda.
exampleTranslation: 气候变化在国际政策议程中占有重要位置。

definition: A whole formed by combining several elements
exampleSentence: The aggregate of the data supports the hypothesis.
exampleTranslation: 数据的总和支持了该假设。

definition: The science or practice of farming and cultivating land
exampleSentence: Agriculture accounts for a significant portion of the economy.
exampleTranslation: 农业占经济的很大一部分。

definition: Although; even though used to introduce a concession
exampleSentence: The results were promising, albeit preliminary.
exampleTranslation: 结果是有希望的，尽管是初步的。

definition: To place or arrange in a straight line or correct position
exampleSentence: The curriculum was aligned with national education standards.
exampleTranslation: 课程与国家教育标准保持一致。

definition: To claim that something is the case without providing proof
exampleSentence: Critics alleged that the study's methodology was flawed.
exampleTranslation: 批评者声称该研究的方法论存在缺陷。

definition: To distribute resources for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The government allocated funds for environmental protection.
exampleTranslation: 政府拨出资金用于环境保护。

definition: To change or cause to change in character or composition
exampleSentence: Human activity has dramatically altered natural habitats.
exampleTranslation: 人类活动极大地改变了自然栖息地。

definition: The height of an object above sea level or ground level
exampleSentence: At high altitudes, atmospheric pressure decreases significantly.
exampleTranslation: 在高海拔地区，大气压力显著降低。

definition: Open to more than one interpretation; unclear
exampleSentence: The results of the experiment were ambiguous.
exampleTranslation: 实验结果模棱两可。

definition: To make changes to a text or law to improve it
exampleSentence: The legislature voted to amend the existing statute.
exampleTranslation: 立法机关投票修改了现有法规。

definition: Enough or more than enough; plentiful
exampleSentence: There is ample evidence to support evolutionary theory.
exampleTranslation: 有充足的证据支持进化论。

definition: A comparison between things for the purpose of explanation
exampleSentence: The professor drew an analogy between the brain and a computer.
exampleTranslation: 教授将大脑和计算机做了类比。

definition: To examine something methodically and in detail
exampleSentence: Scientists analyzed the data using statistical methods.
exampleTranslation: 科学家使用统计方法分析了数据。

definition: A person or organism from whom one is descended
exampleSentence: Humans and apes share a common ancestor.
exampleTranslation: 人类和猿类有一个共同的祖先。

definition: Belonging to the very distant past in history
exampleSentence: Ancient civilizations developed sophisticated irrigation systems.
exampleTranslation: 古代文明发展了精密的灌溉系统。

definition: Occurring once every year; yearly
exampleSentence: The annual rainfall in this region averages 800 millimeters.
exampleTranslation: 该地区年平均降水量为800毫米。

definition: Something that deviates from what is standard or expected
exampleSentence: The temperature anomaly puzzled climate scientists.
exampleTranslation: 温度异常让气候科学家感到困惑。

definition: The study of human societies, cultures, and development
exampleSentence: Anthropology examines cultural practices across societies.
exampleTranslation: 人类学研究不同社会的文化习俗。

definition: To regard as probable; to expect or predict
exampleSentence: Researchers anticipate a rise in global temperatures.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员预计全球温度会上升。

definition: Equipment or machinery needed for a particular activity
exampleSentence: The laboratory apparatus was carefully calibrated.
exampleTranslation: 实验室器械经过了仔细校准。

definition: Clearly visible or understood; obvious
exampleSentence: The effects of pollution are apparent in the water quality.
exampleTranslation: 污染的影响在水质中显而易见。

definition: To make a serious or urgent request; to be attractive
exampleSentence: The conservation group appealed for public support.
exampleTranslation: 环保组织呼吁公众支持。

definition: Supplementary material at the end of a book or document
exampleSentence: Detailed data tables are provided in the appendix.
exampleTranslation: 详细的数据表格在附录中提供。

definition: To recognize the full worth or significance of something
exampleSentence: Students must appreciate the complexity of ecological systems.
exampleTranslation: 学生必须理解生态系统的复杂性。

definition: A way of dealing with something; a method
exampleSentence: A multidisciplinary approach is needed to solve this problem.
exampleTranslation: 解决这个问题需要多学科的方法。

definition: Suitable or proper in the circumstances
exampleSentence: It is important to use appropriate research methodology.
exampleTranslation: 使用适当的研究方法很重要。

definition: Close to the actual but not completely accurate
exampleSentence: The approximate age of the fossil is two million years.
exampleTranslation: 该化石的大约年龄为两百万年。

definition: Relating to or living in water
exampleSentence: Aquatic ecosystems are vulnerable to industrial pollution.
exampleTranslation: 水生生态系统容易受到工业污染的影响。

definition: Based on random choice rather than reason or system
exampleSentence: The classification system appeared somewhat arbitrary.
exampleTranslation: 该分类系统显得有些武断。

definition: The study of human history through excavation of artifacts
exampleSentence: Archaeology has revealed much about ancient trade routes.
exampleTranslation: 考古学揭示了许多关于古代贸易路线的信息。

definition: The art or practice of designing buildings and structures
exampleSentence: Gothic architecture is characterized by pointed arches and vaults.
exampleTranslation: 哥特式建筑以尖拱和拱顶为特征。

definition: Having very little rainfall; extremely dry
exampleSentence: Arid regions receive less than 250 millimeters of annual rainfall.
exampleTranslation: 干旱地区年降水量不足250毫米。

definition: To express an idea or feeling fluently and coherently
exampleSentence: The student articulated her thesis with great clarity.
exampleTranslation: 该学生非常清晰地阐述了她的论文。

definition: An object made by a human being of historical interest
exampleSentence: Artifacts from the excavation date back to 3000 BCE.
exampleTranslation: 出土的文物可以追溯到公元前3000年。

definition: To go up or climb; to rise to a higher level
exampleSentence: The trail ascends steeply through the alpine meadow.
exampleTranslation: 小路陡峭地穿过高山草甸向上延伸。

definition: To attribute something to a particular cause or source
exampleSentence: Historians ascribe the empire's decline to internal corruption.
exampleTranslation: 历史学家将帝国的衰落归因于内部腐败。

definition: A particular part or feature of something
exampleSentence: Every aspect of the experiment was carefully controlled.
exampleTranslation: 实验的每个方面都经过了仔细控制。

definition: To direct one's hopes or ambitions toward achieving something
exampleSentence: Many young scientists aspire to make groundbreaking discoveries.
exampleTranslation: 许多年轻科学家渴望做出突破性的发现。

definition: To gather together in one place or fit parts together
exampleSentence: The researchers assembled a comprehensive database.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员组建了一个综合数据库。

definition: To state a fact or belief confidently and forcefully
exampleSentence: The author asserts that climate change is accelerating.
exampleTranslation: 作者断言气候变化正在加速。

definition: To evaluate or estimate the nature or quality of something
exampleSentence: Scientists assess environmental risks using various models.
exampleTranslation: 科学家使用各种模型评估环境风险。

definition: To allocate a task, role, or duty to someone
exampleSentence: Students were assigned to different research groups.
exampleTranslation: 学生们被分配到不同的研究小组。

definition: To absorb and integrate ideas, culture, or nutrients
exampleSentence: Immigrants gradually assimilate into the host culture.
exampleTranslation: 移民逐渐融入东道国文化。

definition: To help or support someone in doing something
exampleSentence: Technology can assist researchers in data collection.
exampleTranslation: 技术可以协助研究人员进行数据收集。

definition: To connect something in one's mind with something else
exampleSentence: Researchers associate this gene with increased disease risk.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员将这个基因与疾病风险增加联系起来。

definition: To suppose to be the case without proof
exampleSentence: We cannot assume that correlation implies causation.
exampleTranslation: 我们不能假设相关性意味着因果关系。

definition: The scientific study of celestial objects and the universe
exampleSentence: Astronomy has advanced rapidly with new telescope technology.
exampleTranslation: 随着新望远镜技术的发展，天文学取得了快速进步。

definition: The envelope of gases surrounding the earth or a planet
exampleSentence: Carbon dioxide levels in the atmosphere continue to rise.
exampleTranslation: 大气中的二氧化碳含量持续上升。

definition: To fasten, join, or connect one thing to another
exampleSentence: Researchers attached sensors to monitor wildlife migration.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员安装传感器来监测野生动物迁徙。

definition: To succeed in achieving something after effort
exampleSentence: Few students attain fluency in a second language by age ten.
exampleTranslation: 很少有学生在十岁之前达到第二语言的流利程度。

definition: To regard something as being caused by a particular factor
exampleSentence: Scientists attribute the warming trend to greenhouse gases.
exampleTranslation: 科学家将变暖趋势归因于温室气体。

definition: An official inspection of an organization's accounts
exampleSentence: An environmental audit revealed significant pollution levels.
exampleTranslation: 一次环境审查揭示了严重的污染水平。

definition: Of undisputed origin; genuine and real
exampleSentence: The museum verified that the artifact was authentic.
exampleTranslation: 博物馆证实了该文物是真品。

definition: The power or right to give orders and enforce obedience
exampleSentence: The research was published by a leading authority in biology.
exampleTranslation: 该研究由生物学领域的权威人士发表。

definition: Having self-governance; acting independently
exampleSentence: The region declared itself an autonomous territory.
exampleTranslation: 该地区宣布自己为自治领土。

definition: Able to be used or obtained; at one's disposal
exampleSentence: Limited resources are available for conservation efforts.
exampleTranslation: 用于保护工作的资源有限。

definition: An imaginary line around which an object rotates
exampleSentence: The Earth rotates on its axis once every twenty-four hours.
exampleTranslation: 地球每二十四小时绕其轴自转一次。

definition: Microscopic single-celled organisms found everywhere
exampleSentence: Certain bacteria play a vital role in decomposing organic matter.
exampleTranslation: 某些细菌在分解有机物方面发挥着重要作用。

definition: Too poor in quality to produce vegetation; unproductive
exampleSentence: The barren landscape showed signs of severe soil erosion.
exampleTranslation: 贫瘠的地貌显示出严重土壤侵蚀的迹象。

definition: An obstacle that prevents movement or access
exampleSentence: Mountain ranges can serve as barriers to species migration.
exampleTranslation: 山脉可以作为物种迁徙的障碍。

definition: A large natural depression on the earth's surface
exampleSentence: The Amazon Basin contains the world's largest tropical rainforest.
exampleTranslation: 亚马逊盆地拥有世界上最大的热带雨林。

definition: In the interest of a person, group, or principle
exampleSentence: The organization acts on behalf of endangered species.
exampleTranslation: 该组织代表濒危物种采取行动。

definition: A standard or point of reference against which things are compared
exampleSentence: The study established a benchmark for future climate research.
exampleTranslation: 该研究为未来的气候研究建立了基准。

definition: At a lower level or layer than something; under
exampleSentence: Fossils were discovered beneath several layers of sediment.
exampleTranslation: 化石在几层沉积物之下被发现。

definition: Resulting in good; favorable or advantageous
exampleSentence: Moderate exercise is beneficial to cardiovascular health.
exampleTranslation: 适度运动对心血管健康有益。

definition: Prejudice in favor of or against something; systematic error
exampleSentence: Researchers must minimize bias in their experimental design.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员必须在实验设计中尽量减少偏差。

definition: The variety of life in a particular habitat or ecosystem
exampleSentence: Tropical rainforests exhibit the highest levels of biodiversity.
exampleTranslation: 热带雨林展现出最高水平的生物多样性。

definition: Very strange or unusual in appearance or character
exampleSentence: Some deep-sea creatures have bizarre physical adaptations.
exampleTranslation: 一些深海生物有奇异的身体适应性。

definition: To support, strengthen, or reinforce something
exampleSentence: New evidence bolstered the theory of plate tectonics.
exampleTranslation: 新证据增强了板块构造理论。

definition: A force or connection that unites people or atoms
exampleSentence: Chemical bonds hold molecules together in stable configurations.
exampleTranslation: 化学键将分子固定在稳定的构型中。

definition: A period of rapid economic growth or increase
exampleSentence: The post-war economic boom transformed industrial societies.
exampleTranslation: 战后经济繁荣改变了工业社会。

definition: A line that marks the limit of an area; a dividing line
exampleSentence: Tectonic plate boundaries are often sites of seismic activity.
exampleTranslation: 构造板块边界通常是地震活动的场所。

definition: To produce offspring; to rear and raise animals
exampleSentence: Certain bird species breed exclusively on remote islands.
exampleTranslation: 某些鸟类物种只在偏远岛屿上繁殖。

definition: An estimate of income and expenditure for a set period
exampleSentence: The federal budget allocated more resources to scientific research.
exampleTranslation: 联邦预算为科学研究分配了更多资源。

definition: The mass or magnitude of something large; the majority
exampleSentence: The bulk of the evidence supports the evolutionary model.
exampleTranslation: 大部分证据支持进化模型。

definition: Able to stay afloat; cheerful and optimistic
exampleSentence: Oil is buoyant and floats on the surface of water.
exampleTranslation: 油具有浮力，漂浮在水面上。

definition: A system of government by many officials in departments
exampleSentence: Excessive bureaucracy can hinder scientific research progress.
exampleTranslation: 过度的官僚主义会阻碍科学研究的进展。

definition: To determine an amount or number mathematically
exampleSentence: Astronomers calculated the distance to the nearest star.
exampleTranslation: 天文学家计算了到最近恒星的距离。

definition: To adjust a measuring instrument to a known standard
exampleSentence: The instruments must be calibrated before each experiment.
exampleTranslation: 每次实验前必须校准仪器。

definition: A person or thing regarded as suitable for a position
exampleSentence: Several candidate genes have been identified in the study.
exampleTranslation: 该研究已确定了几个候选基因。

definition: Having the ability or quality to do something
exampleSentence: Modern telescopes are capable of detecting distant galaxies.
exampleTranslation: 现代望远镜能够探测到遥远的星系。

definition: The maximum amount something can contain or produce
exampleSentence: The reservoir has a capacity of fifty million cubic meters.
exampleTranslation: 水库容量为五千万立方米。

definition: A chemical element fundamental to all known life forms
exampleSentence: Carbon is the basis of organic chemistry and all living organisms.
exampleTranslation: 碳是有机化学和所有活生物体的基础。

definition: An event causing great and sudden damage or suffering
exampleSentence: The asteroid impact was a catastrophe that led to mass extinction.
exampleTranslation: 小行星撞击是导致大规模灭绝的灾难。

definition: A class or division of things with shared characteristics
exampleSentence: Species are organized into categories based on shared traits.
exampleTranslation: 物种根据共同特征被归入不同类别。

definition: Care taken to avoid danger or mistakes; a warning
exampleSentence: The findings should be interpreted with caution.
exampleTranslation: 这些发现应谨慎解读。

definition: To come or bring to an end; to stop happening
exampleSentence: Volcanic activity in the region ceased approximately 10,000 years ago.
exampleTranslation: 该地区的火山活动大约在一万年前停止。

definition: An official count of a population and its characteristics
exampleSentence: The national census provides essential demographic data.
exampleTranslation: 国家人口普查提供了重要的人口统计数据。

definition: A passage for water or communication; a medium
exampleSentence: Rivers carve channels through rock over thousands of years.
exampleTranslation: 河流在数千年中在岩石中冲刷出河道。

definition: Complete disorder and confusion; unpredictable behavior
exampleSentence: Chaos theory explains sensitivity to initial conditions.
exampleTranslation: 混沌理论解释了对初始条件的敏感性。

definition: A graphical representation of data or a map
exampleSentence: The chart illustrates population growth over the past century.
exampleTranslation: 该图表展示了过去一个世纪的人口增长。

definition: Persisting for a long time or recurring frequently
exampleSentence: Chronic exposure to pollutants can cause respiratory diseases.
exampleTranslation: 长期接触污染物会导致呼吸系统疾病。

definition: Arranged in the order of time of occurrence
exampleSentence: The events are presented in chronological order.
exampleTranslation: 事件按时间顺序呈现。

definition: To move continuously through a closed system or area
exampleSentence: Blood circulates through the body via arteries and veins.
exampleTranslation: 血液通过动脉和静脉在体内循环。

definition: A condition or fact that affects a situation
exampleSentence: Under certain circumstances, species may evolve rapidly.
exampleTranslation: 在某些情况下，物种可能会快速进化。

definition: To refer to a source as evidence for an argument
exampleSentence: The paper cites over fifty peer-reviewed studies.
exampleTranslation: 该论文引用了五十多篇同行评审的研究。

definition: Relating to a city or town and its citizens
exampleSentence: Civic participation is essential for a functioning democracy.
exampleTranslation: 公民参与对运作良好的民主至关重要。

definition: An advanced state of human social and cultural development
exampleSentence: The Mesopotamian civilization is considered one of the earliest.
exampleTranslation: 美索不达米亚文明被认为是最早的文明之一。

definition: To make a statement or situation less confused and clearer
exampleSentence: The researcher clarified the methodology used in the study.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员阐明了研究中使用的方法。

definition: Judged to be of the highest quality; typical
exampleSentence: This is a classic example of natural selection in action.
exampleTranslation: 这是自然选择的经典例子。

definition: To arrange into categories or groups based on qualities
exampleSentence: Biologists classify organisms using a hierarchical taxonomy.
exampleTranslation: 生物学家使用分层分类法对生物体进行分类。

definition: A distinct article or provision in a document or law
exampleSentence: The treaty includes a clause on environmental protection.
exampleTranslation: 该条约包含一项关于环境保护的条款。

definition: A fine-grained natural soil material that is plastic when wet
exampleSentence: Ancient pottery was crafted from locally sourced clay.
exampleTranslation: 古代陶器是用当地的黏土制作的。

definition: The long-term weather conditions prevailing in an area
exampleSentence: Climate change poses a significant threat to global ecosystems.
exampleTranslation: 气候变化对全球生态系统构成重大威胁。

definition: A group of similar things occurring closely together
exampleSentence: Stars often form in dense clusters within nebulae.
exampleTranslation: 恒星经常在星云中形成密集的星团。

definition: An alliance of distinct parties for joint action
exampleSentence: A coalition of nations agreed to reduce carbon emissions.
exampleTranslation: 多国联盟同意减少碳排放。

definition: To persuade by using force or threats
exampleSentence: Authoritarian regimes coerce compliance through intimidation.
exampleTranslation: 专制政权通过恐吓强迫服从。

definition: To exist at the same time or in the same place
exampleSentence: Multiple species coexist within the same ecosystem.
exampleTranslation: 多个物种在同一生态系统中共存。

definition: Relating to the mental processes of perception and learning
exampleSentence: Cognitive development in children follows predictable stages.
exampleTranslation: 儿童的认知发展遵循可预测的阶段。

definition: Logical and consistent; forming a unified whole
exampleSentence: The argument must be coherent and supported by evidence.
exampleTranslation: 论点必须连贯且有证据支持。

definition: To occur at the same time or correspond exactly

definition: To have a mutual relationship or connection between things

definition: To bring something into existence; to produce

definition: To deny a person the possession or use of something

definition: To distribute or spread over a wide area
exampleSentence: Seeds are dispersed by wind, water, and animals.
exampleTranslation: 种子通过风、水和动物传播。

definition: To move something from its proper or usual position
exampleSentence: Rising sea levels may displace millions of coastal residents.
exampleTranslation: 海平面上升可能使数百万沿海居民流离失所。

definition: To show or exhibit something for others to see
exampleSentence: The museum displays artifacts from the Bronze Age.
exampleTranslation: 博物馆展示了青铜时代的文物。

definition: To get rid of by throwing away or destroying
exampleSentence: Hazardous waste must be disposed of properly.
exampleTranslation: 危险废物必须妥善处理。

definition: A disagreement or argument about something important
exampleSentence: The dispute over territorial boundaries lasted for decades.
exampleTranslation: 领土边界争端持续了数十年。

definition: To become incorporated into a liquid so as to form a solution
exampleSentence: Salt dissolves readily in water at room temperature.
exampleTranslation: 盐在室温下很容易溶解在水中。

definition: Recognizably different from others; separate
exampleSentence: The two species occupy distinct ecological niches.
exampleTranslation: 这两个物种占据不同的生态位。

definition: To spread or hand out to several people or areas
exampleSentence: Rainfall is unevenly distributed across the continent.
exampleTranslation: 降水在大陆上分布不均。

definition: To separate and go in different directions; to differ
exampleSentence: The evolutionary paths of the two species diverged millions of years ago.
exampleTranslation: 两个物种的进化路径在数百万年前分道扬镳。

definition: Showing a great deal of variety; very different
exampleSentence: Coral reefs are among the most diverse ecosystems on Earth.
exampleTranslation: 珊瑚礁是地球上最多样化的生态系统之一。

definition: A belief or set of beliefs held and taught by a group
exampleSentence: The Monroe Doctrine shaped American foreign policy for decades.
exampleTranslation: 门罗主义塑造了美国几十年的外交政策。

definition: To record or report in detail for future reference
exampleSentence: Researchers documented the behavioral patterns of the species.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员记录了该物种的行为模式。

definition: An area of territory or a field of activity or knowledge
exampleSentence: Molecular biology has become an important scientific domain.
exampleTranslation: 分子生物学已成为一个重要的科学领域。

definition: Relating to the running of a home or one's own country
exampleSentence: Domestic policies can have significant global implications.
exampleTranslation: 国内政策可能具有重大的全球影响。

definition: To tame an animal or cultivate a plant for human use
exampleSentence: Humans domesticated wheat approximately 10,000 years ago.
exampleTranslation: 人类大约在一万年前驯化了小麦。

definition: Most influential, powerful, or prominent
exampleSentence: The dominant species in the ecosystem determines community structure.
exampleTranslation: 生态系统中的优势物种决定了群落结构。

definition: To give money or goods for a good cause or charity
exampleSentence: The foundation donated funds for archaeological research.
exampleTranslation: 基金会捐赠了考古研究资金。

definition: In a state of rest or inactivity; temporarily inactive
exampleSentence: The volcano has been dormant for over a thousand years.
exampleTranslation: 这座火山已经休眠了一千多年。

definition: A preliminary version of a piece of writing or a plan
exampleSentence: The draft of the international treaty was reviewed by experts.
exampleTranslation: 国际条约的草案由专家审查。

definition: To cause liquid to flow away from something
exampleSentence: Excessive irrigation can drain underground aquifers.
exampleTranslation: 过度灌溉会耗尽地下蓄水层。

definition: A literary genre of stories told through performance
exampleSentence: Greek drama explored fundamental questions about human nature.
exampleTranslation: 希腊戏剧探索了关于人性的基本问题。

definition: Likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect; severe
exampleSentence: Drastic measures are needed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
exampleTranslation: 需要采取激烈措施来减少温室气体排放。

definition: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall
exampleSentence: Severe drought can lead to widespread crop failure.
exampleTranslation: 严重的干旱可导致大范围作物歉收。

definition: The time during which something continues or lasts
exampleSentence: The duration of the experiment was six months.
exampleTranslation: 实验持续时间为六个月。

definition: Characterized by constant change and activity
exampleSentence: Ecosystems are dynamic systems that constantly evolve.
exampleTranslation: 生态系统是不断演变的动态系统。

definition: A sudden shaking of the ground caused by tectonic movement
exampleSentence: The earthquake measured 7.8 on the Richter scale.
exampleTranslation: 地震在里氏震级上测量为7.8级。

definition: An obscuring of light from one celestial body by another
exampleSentence: A solar eclipse occurs when the moon passes between Earth and the sun.
exampleTranslation: 当月球经过地球和太阳之间时，就会发生日食。

definition: The branch of biology dealing with organisms and environments
exampleSentence: Ecology examines the relationships between organisms and habitats.
exampleTranslation: 生态学研究生物体与栖息地之间的关系。

definition: A biological community of interacting organisms and environment
exampleSentence: Coral reef ecosystems support an enormous variety of marine life.
exampleTranslation: 珊瑚礁生态系统支撑着种类繁多的海洋生物。

definition: Involving many carefully arranged parts; detailed
exampleSentence: The researcher provided an elaborate explanation of the methodology.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员对方法论做了详尽的解释。

definition: A component or part; a substance that cannot be decomposed
exampleSentence: Hydrogen is the lightest chemical element in the periodic table.
exampleTranslation: 氢是元素周期表中最轻的化学元素。

definition: To raise to a higher position or level
exampleSentence: The discovery elevated the field to mainstream scientific attention.
exampleTranslation: 这一发现将该领域提升到了主流科学的关注范围。

definition: To draw out a response or reaction from someone
exampleSentence: The survey was designed to elicit honest responses from participants.
exampleTranslation: 该调查旨在引出参与者的诚实回答。

definition: To completely remove or get rid of something
exampleSentence: The study aimed to eliminate all potential sources of error.
exampleTranslation: 该研究旨在消除所有潜在的误差来源。

definition: To fix something firmly and deeply in a surrounding mass
exampleSentence: Fossils are often embedded in layers of sedimentary rock.
exampleTranslation: 化石通常嵌入在沉积岩层中。

definition: To accept willingly; to hold closely in one's arms
exampleSentence: The scientific community embraced the new methodology.
exampleTranslation: 科学界接受了新的方法论。

definition: An unborn offspring in the early stages of development
exampleSentence: The embryo develops rapidly during the first trimester.
exampleTranslation: 胚胎在第一个三个月内发育迅速。

definition: To come into view or existence; to become apparent
exampleSentence: New evidence has emerged to support the theory.
exampleTranslation: 新证据已经出现以支持该理论。

definition: The production and discharge of something, especially gas
exampleSentence: Carbon emissions from transportation are a major concern.
exampleTranslation: 交通运输的碳排放是一个主要问题。

definition: Special importance or significance given to something
exampleSentence: The curriculum places emphasis on critical thinking skills.
exampleTranslation: 课程强调批判性思维能力。

definition: Based on observation or experience rather than theory
exampleSentence: Empirical evidence is essential for validating scientific theories.
exampleTranslation: 实证证据对于验证科学理论至关重要。

definition: To give someone the authority or means to do something
exampleSentence: Technology enables researchers to collect data more efficiently.
exampleTranslation: 技术使研究人员能够更高效地收集数据。

definition: To surround or hold within; to include comprehensively
exampleSentence: The study encompasses data from over 50 countries.
exampleTranslation: 该研究涵盖了来自50多个国家的数据。

definition: To unexpectedly meet or experience something
exampleSentence: Explorers encountered diverse ecosystems during the expedition.
exampleTranslation: 探险家在考察期间遇到了多样的生态系统。

definition: To put someone or something at risk of harm
exampleSentence: Pollution continues to endanger marine wildlife populations.
exampleTranslation: 污染继续危及海洋野生动物种群。

definition: An attempt to achieve a goal; a serious effort
exampleSentence: Space exploration is a complex scientific endeavor.
exampleTranslation: 太空探索是一项复杂的科学事业。

definition: To declare one's public approval or support of something
exampleSentence: The panel endorsed the findings of the environmental report.
exampleTranslation: 小组认可了环境报告的调查结果。

definition: To suffer through something difficult patiently over time
exampleSentence: Ancient structures that endure for millennia reveal superior engineering.
exampleTranslation: 历经千年不衰的古代建筑揭示了卓越的工程技术。

definition: The capacity for doing work; power derived from resources
exampleSentence: Solar energy is becoming an increasingly viable alternative.
exampleTranslation: 太阳能正成为一种越来越可行的替代能源。

definition: To participate or become involved in an activity
exampleSentence: Students must actively engage with the course material.
exampleTranslation: 学生必须积极参与课程材料。

definition: To intensify, increase, or improve the quality of something
exampleSentence: Fertilizers enhance soil productivity for agriculture.
exampleTranslation: 肥料提高了土壤的农业生产力。

definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent
exampleSentence: The universe contains an enormous number of galaxies.
exampleTranslation: 宇宙包含数量庞大的星系。

definition: To improve the quality or value of something
exampleSentence: Volcanic ash enriches the surrounding soil with minerals.
exampleTranslation: 火山灰用矿物质丰富了周围的土壤。

definition: A project or undertaking; a business or company
exampleSentence: Scientific research is a collaborative enterprise.
exampleTranslation: 科学研究是一项合作事业。

definition: A thing with distinct and independent existence
exampleSentence: A corporation is a legal entity separate from its owners.
exampleTranslation: 公司是与其所有者分开的法律实体。

definition: A person who sets up a business taking on financial risk
exampleSentence: Entrepreneurs drive innovation in the renewable energy sector.
exampleTranslation: 企业家推动可再生能源领域的创新。

definition: The surroundings in which an organism lives and operates
exampleSentence: The environment plays a crucial role in species adaptation.
exampleTranslation: 环境在物种适应中起着至关重要的作用。

definition: To imagine or expect something as a future possibility
exampleSentence: Scientists envision a future powered entirely by renewables.
exampleTranslation: 科学家设想一个完全由可再生能源驱动的未来。

definition: An event or period considered in isolation; an incident
exampleSentence: The ice age was a significant episode in Earth's geological history.
exampleTranslation: 冰河时代是地球地质历史上的重要事件。

definition: A period of time in history marked by notable events
exampleSentence: The Holocene epoch began approximately 11,700 years ago.
exampleTranslation: 全新世纪元大约始于11700年前。

definition: To consider one thing as equal to another
exampleSentence: One should not equate economic growth with social progress.
exampleTranslation: 人们不应将经济增长等同于社会进步。

definition: An imaginary line around the middle of the earth
exampleSentence: Temperatures near the equator remain relatively constant year-round.
exampleTranslation: 赤道附近的温度全年保持相对恒定。

definition: A state of balance between opposing forces or elements
exampleSentence: Chemical equilibrium is reached when reaction rates are equal.
exampleTranslation: 当反应速率相等时，化学平衡就达到了。

definition: To supply with the necessary items for a purpose
exampleSentence: The laboratory is equipped with state-of-the-art instruments.
exampleTranslation: 该实验室配备了最先进的仪器。

definition: Equal in value, function, or meaning to something else
exampleSentence: One kilogram is equivalent to approximately 2.2 pounds.
exampleTranslation: 一公斤大约等于2.2磅。

definition: A long and distinct period of history
exampleSentence: The Industrial Era transformed the economic landscape.
exampleTranslation: 工业时代改变了经济格局。

definition: To completely destroy or remove something
exampleSentence: Vaccination campaigns have eradicated smallpox worldwide.
exampleTranslation: 疫苗接种运动已在全球范围内根除了天花。

definition: To gradually wear away by wind, water, or other forces
exampleSentence: Coastal cliffs are continuously eroded by wave action.
exampleTranslation: 海岸悬崖不断被海浪侵蚀。

definition: The gradual wearing away of rock, soil, or land surface
exampleSentence: Soil erosion is accelerated by deforestation and overgrazing.
exampleTranslation: 土壤侵蚀因森林砍伐和过度放牧而加速。

definition: Not even or regular in pattern; unpredictable
exampleSentence: Erratic weather patterns have disrupted agricultural cycles.
exampleTranslation: 不稳定的天气模式扰乱了农业周期。

definition: Absolutely necessary; extremely important
exampleSentence: Water is essential for all forms of life on Earth.
exampleTranslation: 水对地球上所有生命形式都是必不可少的。

definition: To set up or found an organization, system, or principle
exampleSentence: The university established a new center for climate research.
exampleTranslation: 大学建立了一个新的气候研究中心。

definition: To roughly calculate the value or quantity of something
exampleSentence: Scientists estimate that the universe is 13.8 billion years old.
exampleTranslation: 科学家估计宇宙已有138亿年的历史。

definition: Relating to a group sharing cultural or national traditions
exampleSentence: Ethnic diversity enriches the cultural fabric of a society.
exampleTranslation: 民族多样性丰富了社会的文化结构。

definition: To judge or determine the significance or quality of something
exampleSentence: Peer reviewers evaluate the methodology of submitted papers.
exampleTranslation: 同行评审员评估提交论文的方法论。

definition: To turn from liquid into vapor; to disappear gradually
exampleSentence: Water evaporates from the ocean surface and forms clouds.
exampleTranslation: 水从海洋表面蒸发并形成云。

definition: Occurring at the end of a process; ultimate
exampleSentence: The eventual outcome of the experiment confirmed the hypothesis.
exampleTranslation: 实验的最终结果证实了假设。

definition: The available body of facts indicating whether a belief is true
exampleSentence: Fossil evidence supports the theory of continental drift.
exampleTranslation: 化石证据支持大陆漂移理论。

definition: To develop gradually over successive generations
exampleSentence: Species evolve through the process of natural selection.
exampleTranslation: 物种通过自然选择的过程进化。

definition: To represent something as greater than it actually is
exampleSentence: Media reports sometimes exaggerate the risks of new technologies.
exampleTranslation: 媒体报道有时会夸大新技术的风险。

definition: To dig out and remove earth carefully from an area
exampleSentence: Archaeologists excavated ruins dating to the Roman period.
exampleTranslation: 考古学家发掘了追溯到罗马时期的遗迹。

definition: To be greater than a number or amount; to surpass
exampleSentence: Global temperatures may exceed pre-industrial levels by two degrees.
exampleTranslation: 全球温度可能比工业化前水平高出两度。

definition: A short extract from a film, broadcast, or piece of writing
exampleSentence: The textbook includes excerpts from primary historical sources.
exampleTranslation: 教科书包含来自原始历史资料的摘录。

definition: An amount that is more than necessary or permitted
exampleSentence: An excess of nutrients in water can cause algal blooms.
exampleTranslation: 水中营养物质过量会导致藻类大量繁殖。

definition: To deny access to or prevent from being included
exampleSentence: The study excluded participants with pre-existing conditions.
exampleTranslation: 该研究排除了有既往疾病的参与者。

definition: To carry out or put into effect a plan or action
exampleSentence: The team executed the experiment according to the protocol.
exampleTranslation: 团队按照协议执行了实验。

definition: To serve as a typical example of something
exampleSentence: This case study exemplifies the challenges of urban planning.
exampleTranslation: 这个案例研究例证了城市规划的挑战。

definition: Free from an obligation or liability imposed on others
exampleSentence: Non-profit organizations are often exempt from taxation.
exampleTranslation: 非营利组织通常免税。

definition: To apply or bring to bear a force or influence
exampleSentence: Gravity exerts a force on all objects with mass.
exampleTranslation: 重力对所有有质量的物体施加力。

definition: To drain of strength or resources; to use up completely
exampleSentence: The prolonged drought exhausted the region's water reserves.
exampleTranslation: 长期干旱耗尽了该地区的水储备。

definition: To publicly display or show a quality or characteristic
exampleSentence: Some minerals exhibit fluorescence under ultraviolet light.
exampleTranslation: 一些矿物在紫外光下展现出荧光。

definition: To become or make larger in size, number, or importance
exampleSentence: The universe continues to expand at an accelerating rate.
exampleTranslation: 宇宙继续以加速的速率膨胀。

definition: A journey undertaken for a specific purpose
exampleSentence: The Arctic expedition collected valuable climate data.
exampleTranslation: 北极探险收集了有价值的气候数据。

definition: The action of spending money or using resources
exampleSentence: Government expenditure on education has increased steadily.
exampleTranslation: 政府在教育上的支出稳步增加。

definition: A scientific procedure to test a hypothesis
exampleSentence: The experiment was designed to test the effects of temperature.
exampleTranslation: 该实验旨在测试温度的影响。

definition: Expert skill or knowledge in a particular field
exampleSentence: The project required expertise in both biology and statistics.
exampleTranslation: 该项目需要生物学和统计学方面的专业知识。

definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for doubt
exampleSentence: The instructions must be explicit to avoid misinterpretation.
exampleTranslation: 说明必须明确以避免误解。

definition: To make full use of a resource; to use unfairly
exampleSentence: Many nations exploit their natural resources unsustainably.
exampleTranslation: 许多国家不可持续地开发其自然资源。

definition: Becoming more and more rapid at an increasing rate
exampleSentence: The exponential growth of the population strains natural resources.
exampleTranslation: 人口的指数增长使自然资源紧张。

definition: To send goods or services to another country for sale
exampleSentence: The nation exports a significant volume of agricultural products.
exampleTranslation: 该国出口大量农产品。

definition: To make something visible or subject to something
exampleSentence: Overexposure to sunlight can damage skin cells.
exampleTranslation: 过度暴露在阳光下会损伤皮肤细胞。

definition: To cause to cover a larger area; to stretch out
exampleSentence: The forest extends across thousands of square kilometers.
exampleTranslation: 森林延伸数千平方公里。

definition: Coming from or relating to the outside
exampleSentence: External factors such as climate affect crop yields.
exampleTranslation: 气候等外部因素影响作物产量。

definition: To remove or obtain a substance from something
exampleSentence: Scientists extract DNA from biological samples for analysis.
exampleTranslation: 科学家从生物样本中提取DNA进行分析。

definition: Reaching a high or the highest degree; very severe
exampleSentence: Extreme weather events are becoming more frequent.
exampleTranslation: 极端天气事件正变得更加频繁。

definition: The fundamental structure of a society or system
exampleSentence: Immigration has altered the social fabric of many nations.
exampleTranslation: 移民改变了许多国家的社会结构。

definition: To make an action or process easier or more achievable
exampleSentence: Technology facilitates communication across long distances.
exampleTranslation: 技术促进了远距离的通信。

definition: A circumstance or element that contributes to a result
exampleSentence: Multiple factors contribute to the decline of coral reefs.
exampleTranslation: 多种因素导致了珊瑚礁的衰退。

definition: The teaching staff of a university; an inherent ability
exampleSentence: The faculty includes experts in environmental science.
exampleTranslation: 教师队伍包括环境科学方面的专家。

definition: Extreme scarcity of food in a region
exampleSentence: Prolonged drought can lead to widespread famine.
exampleTranslation: 长期干旱可导致大范围饥荒。

definition: The animals of a particular region or geological period
exampleSentence: The Galápagos Islands are home to unique fauna.
exampleTranslation: 加拉帕戈斯群岛是独特动物群的栖息地。

definition: Possible and practical to achieve or carry out
exampleSentence: The committee assessed whether the plan was feasible.
exampleTranslation: 委员会评估了该计划是否可行。

definition: Relating to a system of government with central authority
exampleSentence: Federal regulations govern the use of protected lands.
exampleTranslation: 联邦法规管理保护区的使用。

definition: Producing or capable of producing abundant vegetation
exampleSentence: The Nile Delta is one of the most fertile regions on Earth.
exampleTranslation: 尼罗河三角洲是地球上最肥沃的地区之一。

definition: A thread or filament from which cloth or tissue is formed
exampleSentence: Plant fibers have been used in textile production for millennia.
exampleTranslation: 植物纤维已被用于纺织生产数千年。

definition: Having limits or bounds; not infinite
exampleSentence: Earth's fossil fuel reserves are finite and will eventually run out.
exampleTranslation: 地球的化石燃料储量是有限的，最终会耗尽。

definition: A defect or imperfection in something
exampleSentence: The flaw in the experimental design compromised the results.
exampleTranslation: 实验设计中的缺陷损害了结果。

definition: The plants of a particular region or geological period
exampleSentence: Researchers cataloged the diverse flora of the tropical forest.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员编录了热带森林中多样的植物群。

definition: To grow or develop in a healthy or vigorous way
exampleSentence: Trade routes allowed ancient civilizations to flourish.
exampleTranslation: 贸易路线使古代文明得以繁荣。

definition: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
exampleSentence: Global temperatures fluctuate due to natural and human factors.
exampleTranslation: 全球温度因自然和人为因素而波动。

definition: A substance that flows freely; a liquid or gas
exampleSentence: Blood is a fluid that transports nutrients throughout the body.
exampleTranslation: 血液是一种在全身运输营养物质的流体。

definition: To concentrate attention or effort on a particular subject
exampleSentence: The study focuses on the impact of urbanization on wildlife.
exampleTranslation: 该研究集中于城市化对野生动物的影响。

definition: The traditional beliefs, customs, and stories of a community
exampleSentence: Folklore often reflects the values of a particular culture.
exampleTranslation: 民间传说常常反映特定文化的价值观。

definition: To predict a future event or trend based on evidence
exampleSentence: Scientists forecast a significant rise in global temperatures.
exampleTranslation: 科学家预测全球温度将显著上升。

definition: To create something strong or successful; to shape metal
exampleSentence: The alliance forged between the two nations lasted centuries.
exampleTranslation: 两国之间建立的联盟持续了几个世纪。

definition: The process of forming or being formed; a structure
exampleSentence: Cloud formation is influenced by temperature and humidity.
exampleTranslation: 云的形成受温度和湿度的影响。

definition: Inspiring fear or respect through size or capability
exampleSentence: The Himalayas present a formidable barrier to weather patterns.
exampleTranslation: 喜马拉雅山脉对天气模式构成了巨大的障碍。

definition: A mathematical or chemical symbolic expression
exampleSentence: Einstein's famous formula relates energy to mass.
exampleTranslation: 爱因斯坦著名的公式将能量与质量联系起来。

definition: The preserved remains of a prehistoric organism
exampleSentence: Fossils provide crucial evidence about past life on Earth.
exampleTranslation: 化石提供了关于地球过去生命的关键证据。

definition: To encourage or promote the development of something
exampleSentence: Universities foster innovation through research funding.
exampleTranslation: 大学通过研究资助促进创新。

definition: The underlying basis or principle for something
exampleSentence: Mathematics serves as the foundation for many scientific fields.
exampleTranslation: 数学是许多科学领域的基础。

definition: A small or tiny part of something; a numerical quantity
exampleSentence: Only a fraction of the ocean floor has been explored.
exampleTranslation: 只有一小部分海底被探索过。

definition: A small part broken off from something larger
exampleSentence: Bone fragments found at the site date back thousands of years.
exampleTranslation: 在该遗址发现的骨骼碎片可追溯到数千年前。

definition: A basic structure underlying a system or concept
exampleSentence: The theoretical framework guides the research methodology.
exampleTranslation: 理论框架指导研究方法。

definition: The rate at which something occurs over a particular time
exampleSentence: The frequency of extreme weather events has increased.
exampleTranslation: 极端天气事件的频率增加了。

definition: The resistance of one surface moving over another
exampleSentence: Friction between tectonic plates causes earthquakes.
exampleTranslation: 构造板块之间的摩擦引起地震。

definition: The extreme limit of understanding or achievement
exampleSentence: Genetic engineering represents the frontier of modern biology.
exampleTranslation: 基因工程代表了现代生物学的前沿。

definition: Material used to produce heat or power by burning
exampleSentence: Fossil fuels remain the primary source of global energy.
exampleTranslation: 化石燃料仍然是全球能源的主要来源。

definition: The purpose or role of something; a mathematical relation
exampleSentence: The function of chlorophyll is to absorb light energy.
exampleTranslation: 叶绿素的功能是吸收光能。

definition: To provide money for a project or organization
exampleSentence: The government funds basic scientific research annually.
exampleTranslation: 政府每年资助基础科学研究。

definition: Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
exampleSentence: DNA replication is fundamental to all living organisms.
exampleTranslation: DNA复制对所有活生物体来说是根本的。

definition: A spore-producing organism such as a mushroom or mold
exampleSentence: Fungi play an essential role in decomposing organic matter.
exampleTranslation: 真菌在分解有机物方面发挥着重要作用。

definition: To involve oneself deeply in an activity or subject

definition: Resistant to a particular infection or disease

definition: A likely consequence of something; an indirect suggestion

definition: To live in or occupy a place or environment

definition: To hinder, restrain, or prevent an action or process

definition: A new method, idea, or product; the act of innovating

definition: What is put in or contributed to a system or process

definition: To look at something closely to assess its quality

definition: A tool or device used for a particular purpose

definition: To protect from heat, cold, or noise by covering

definition: To combine or be combined to form a unified whole

definition: Relating to the intellect or higher mental functions

definition: Of extreme force, degree, or strength

definition: To act in a way that has an effect on each other

definition: To obstruct and prevent from continuing to a destination

definition: The inner part of something; the inland regions

definition: Coming between two things in time, place, or character

definition: Occurring at irregular intervals; not continuous

definition: Of or situated on the inside; existing within

definition: To explain the meaning of information or an action

definition: A space of time between two events or measurements

definition: To come between so as to prevent or alter a result

definition: Very close and detailed; private and personal

exampleTranslation: 研究人员对当地生态系统有密切的了解。

definition: Very complicated or detailed; having many interrelated parts

exampleTranslation: 珊瑚生长的复杂精细模式令海洋生物学家着迷。

definition: Belonging naturally to something; essential and inherent

definition: To enter forcefully as an enemy; to spread harmfully

definition: To carry out systematic inquiry to establish facts

definition: To cite or appeal to as an authority; to call upon

definition: To include as a necessary part or result

definition: To supply water to land or crops to aid growth

definition: A scholarly publication with peer-reviewed articles

exampleTranslation: 研究结果发表在一家顶尖的科学期刊上。

definition: The official power to make legal decisions in an area

exampleTranslation: 环境法规因管辖权不同而异。

definition: To show or prove to be right or reasonable

exampleTranslation: 研究人员用先前的证据证明了他们的方法论。

exampleTranslation: 作者将古代和现代建筑风格并列比较。

definition: Having or showing eagerness and enthusiasm

exampleTranslation: 科学家们热切地想要探索基因治疗的潜力。

exampleTranslation: 动能随物体速度的增加而增加。

definition: A tag or marker attached to an object for identification

exampleTranslation: 每个标本都贴上了分类标签。

definition: Work, especially physical work; the workforce

exampleTranslation: 工业革命改变了全球的劳动实践。

definition: To fall behind in movement, progress, or development

definition: The visible features of an area of land; a broad view

exampleTranslation: 冰川雕刻了山谷壮观的景观。

definition: The angular distance north or south from the equator

exampleTranslation: 生物多样性通常随纬度增加而减少。

definition: To set in motion or initiate an activity or enterprise

exampleTranslation: NASA发射了一个探测器来研究外太阳系。

definition: A sheet or thickness of material covering a surface

definition: An educational talk to an audience on a particular subject

exampleTranslation: 教授做了一场关于量子力学的讲座。

definition: Relating to or permitted by law

exampleTranslation: 需要法律框架来保护知识产权。

definition: A traditional story sometimes regarded as historical

exampleTranslation: 根据传说，这座城市是由古代英雄建立的。

definition: Laws collectively enacted by a legislative body

exampleTranslation: 环境立法旨在减少工业污染。

definition: Conforming to the law or to rules; valid and justified

exampleTranslation: 研究人员提出了关于数据准确性的正当担忧。

definition: A piece of glass used in optical instruments; a perspective

exampleTranslation: 从文化视角看历史揭示了隐藏的叙事。

definition: To impose a tax, fee, or fine on someone or something

exampleTranslation: 政府对工业排放征收碳税。

definition: Open to new ideas; favoring individual liberty
